text.title: DDD ACL Failure Modes
text.summary: Four antipatterns observed when integrating external systems without a proper ACL. Symptom + root cause + fix per item.

antipattern.symptom: Multiple files import `from stripe import StripeClient` directly. Stripe v8 -> v9 SDK change requires touching 23 files; tests need a Stripe instance to spin up.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treated ACL as optional; SDK imported wherever convenient.
antipattern.fix: Define a `PaymentGateway` interface in domain. Add a single `StripeGateway` adapter in infrastructure. Replace each direct import with the domain interface via DI.

antipattern.symptom: `stripe.error.CardError` thrown by SDK propagates into the application-service handler. Handler catches `Exception`; vendor-specific message ends up in the user-visible error.
antipattern.root-cause: ACL didn't translate; raw vendor exception escaped the boundary.
antipattern.fix: Inside the adapter, `try`/`except` the vendor exceptions and `raise PaymentDeclined(reason=...)` (or whatever the domain models). Test the translation.

antipattern.symptom: Inventory API outage → entire order flow returns 500. Sales paused for the duration of the partner outage.
antipattern.root-cause: ACL doesn't define behaviour when the dependency is unreachable.
antipattern.fix: Pick + document a fail-safe: assume-available with retry, deny-by-default with explicit error message, or circuit-breaker with cached last-known-state. Add a unit test exercising the fail-safe path.

antipattern.symptom: SDK upgrade silently changes the `available_quantity` field to `available_count`. ACL returns `None` for every product; orders fail with no clear error.
antipattern.root-cause: ACL had no pinned contract test against the external system's response shape.
antipattern.fix: Add a contract test using a mock HTTP server (`responses` / `pytest-httpx`) replaying the expected JSON. Fail CI if the actual response shape diverges. Re-record the mock when intentional.
